purpose: Header for PluginWindow, a DocumentWindow subclass that displays a plugin's editor (or a generic fallback) in its own top-level window. Provides static lookup (getWindowFor) by AudioProcessorGraph::Node — one window per (node, format type) pair — plus bulk-close helpers. Free helpers map a WindowFormatType to PropertiesFile keys for restoring last position / open state.
invariants: WindowFormatType enum: Normal, Generic, Programs, Parameters, NumTypes. NumTypes is the sentinel — never use it as an actual format.
getDesktopScaleFactor() is overridden to return 1.0f — JUCE will otherwise scale plugin editors per the system DPI and many plugins render incorrectly under scaling.
PropertiesFile keys ('uiLastX_' + type, 'uiLastY_' + type, 'uiopen_' + type) embed the WindowFormatType name. Renaming any toString() return value invalidates persisted window state for that format.
Static getActiveWindows() returns a singleton Array<PluginWindow*>; no copies, all PluginWindow instances are tracked via raw pointers in this array.
JUCE_DECLARE_NON_COPYABLE_WITH_LEAK_DETECTOR — PluginWindow has identity (the Node::Ptr it owns); copying is meaningless.
gotchas: Window pointers in getActiveWindows() are raw — they are added in the constructor and removed in the destructor. Forgetting to construct via 'new PluginWindow(...)' or destruct via 'delete' will leak or double-free the list entry.
closeButtonPressed() does `delete this;` — the standard JUCE pattern for owned DocumentWindows. Don't keep PluginWindow* in a stack/value container that might also try to free them.
